channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Azerbaijan's president says crashed jetliner was shot down by Russia unintentionally
item.description: Azerbaijan’s President Ilham Aliyev said Sunday that the Azerbaijani airliner that crashed last week was shot down by Russia, albeit unintentionally, and criticized Moscow for trying to “hush up” the issue for days. "We can say with complete clarity that the plane was shot down by Russia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A plane crashes and bursts into flames while landing in South Korea, killing 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A passenger plane skidded off a runway at a South Korean airport Sunday, slammed into a concrete fence and burst into flames after its front landing gear apparently failed to deploy.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Washington state teens charged as adults in gang-linked stabbing of boy, 14, who was tied to tree, cut open
item.description: Milo Canales and Hayden Lusebrink, both 17, are being tried as adults in the alleged stabbing of a 14-year-old old boy who was tied to a tree in a Washington state park and cut open.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colorado man accused of attacking TV reporter, asking him if he's a US citizen
item.description: A Colorado man allegedly attacked a television news reporter after asking if he was a citizen, saying, "This is Trump's America now," court documents show, as reported by the Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: Driver fleeing at 120 mph slams into trooper in Florida
item.description: A driver is facing several charges after fleeing Florida Highway Patrol troopers, and slamming into their patrol vehicle at speeds over 120 mph in Tampa
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: Videos show apparent tornadoes moving through Mississippi
item.description: FRANKLIN COUNTY, Miss. (WJTV) - An apparent tornado moved through Franklin County on Saturday, December 28. Karla Wallace, a WJTV 12 News viewer, submitted a video that showed the storm moving through the McCall Creek area.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Howell, NewsNation

item.title: LDS mission president from Utah dies 'unexpectedly' in France
item.description: A mission president for The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints died on Saturday, Dec. 28 in France, according to a spokesperson for the church.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: These are America's safest small towns: report
item.description: MoneyGeek, a personal finance site, had researchers analyze last year’s FBI crime data of more than 1,000 small towns and cities where the population is between 30,000 and 100,000.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Big Lots reaches deal to keep hundreds of US stores open
item.description: The discount chain Big Lots, which filed for bankruptcy protection in September, has reached a deal that will keep hundreds of its stores and distribution centers open.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, Fox 5

item.title: Musk writes op-ed backing far-right German political party
item.description: The Tesla CEO said he has the “right” to address the country’s political climate as he has “made significant investments” in Germany’s technological and industrial sectors.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Trump weighs in on H-1B debate, backs immigration visas
item.description: “I’ve always liked the visas, I have always been in favor of the visas. That’s why we have them,” Trump said in a phone interview with The New York Post published Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, ABC 4

item.title: After predicting the financial growth of 2024, here's what 2 economists see in 2025
item.description: Brian Blank is a finance scholar and Fed watcher who researches how companies navigate downturns and make financial decisions, as well as how markets process information.
item.dc:creator: KLAS, CBS News

item.title: These songs dominated the Billboard charts longest
item.description: Every now and then, a new song releases and dominates radio airwaves, Spotify playlists, and, of course, the esteemed Billboard Hot 100 chart—and in terms of those types of hits, 2024 has been pretty impressive.
item.dc:creator: Angela Underwood, Stacker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Olivia Hussey, star of 1968 film 'Romeo and Juliet,' dies at 73
item.description: Olivia Hussey, the actor who starred as a teenage Juliet in the 1968 film “Romeo and Juliet,” has died, her family said on social media Saturday. She was 73.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Will New Year's Eve be loud or quiet? What are the top 2025 resolutions? AP-NORC poll has answers
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If you're planning on ringing in the new year quietly at home, you're not alone. A majority of U.S adults intend to celebrate New Year’s Eve at home, according to a new poll by The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Winning $1.22 billion Mega Millions ticket sold in California
item.description: One lucky Californian is the state's newest billionaire after winning numbers for the $1.22 billion Mega Millions jackpot were drawn Friday night. The winning numbers were 3, 7, 37, 49, 55 and the Mega Ball number was 6. The Megaplier was 3X.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KTLA 5

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sinkhole in New Jersey keeps I-80 closed after a section collapses into an abandoned mine
item.description: Road crews have been working 'around the clock' to repair a stretch of Interstate 80 in New Jersey after a sinkhole forced the eastbound lanes to close.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas woman killed in Tyson Foods plant explosion in Georgia, others injured
item.description: An explosion at a Tyson Foods plant in Georgia late Thursday night left one person dead and others injured.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man indicted in burning death of woman inside a New York City subway train, prosecutors say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of burning a sleeping woman to death inside a New York City subway train has been indicted on murder and arson charges, prosecutors announced Friday, as authorities continue working to identify the victim.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Final crystal triangles installed on Times Square ball ahead of New Year's Eve
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The last of the crystal triangles that make up this year's Times Square New Year's Eve ball were installed on Friday morning, marking the first time in 10 years that all 2,688 were replaced at once.
item.dc:creator: BY MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vote to name new baby pygmy hippo at Metro Richmond Zoo
item.description: The Metro Richmond Zoo is asking for the public's help to name its new baby pygmy hippo that the zoo welcomed just before the holidays.
item.dc:creator: Marysa Tuttle, WFLA, NBC

item.title: MAGA civil war erupts between Musk, critics over H-1B visas
item.description: A Trump World civil war has erupted over visas for highly skilled workers, with the president-elect’s new tech industry allies like Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy on one side and the anti-immigration MAGA base on the other.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Another unticketed passenger attempts to stow away on a Delta flight
item.description: In the thick of holiday travel, another unticketed passenger attempted to stow away on a Delta Air Lines flight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bird flu virus in Louisiana patient likely mutated to be more transmissible, CDC says
item.description: The agency, in its latest update released Thursday, said the mutations were not found in samples taken from wild birds, suggesting the virus changed over the course of the patient’s infection.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, ABC 4

item.title: Pennsylvania teacher jailed in Russia formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than four years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: SEE IT: ‘Peanut-shaped’ roundabout ready to open in Ohio
item.description: In a post to the city's Facebook page, officials offered "a few pointers to help drivers navigate safely.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, ABC 4

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Onion-wielding man disrupts Christmas church services, pours whiskey into holy water: deputies
item.description: A Maryland man was arrested and charged with several offences stemming from disruptions at two Catholic churches on Christmas Eve, including one disruption which he is accused of pouring liquor into a container of holy water.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: Kay Granger revelation punctuates increasing scrutiny of aging officials
item.description: The revelation that Rep. Kay Granger (R-Texas) has been living in an assisted living facility is fueling scrutiny of aging public officials, highlighting the shifting norms surrounding lawmaker health, seniority, and for how long it is appropriate to hang on to power.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: LA building from famous rock album cover goes up in flames
item.description: Once featured on the cover of a Doors album, the former site of the Morrison Hotel was destroyed by a fire on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: Manchin objects to Biden's commutations
item.description: Outgoing Sen. Joe Manchin (I-W.Va.) is denouncing as “misguided” and “disgusting” President Biden's move to commute the sentences of two men convicted of murdering a student at Marshall University.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: US homelessness up 18% as affordable housing remains out of reach for many people
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The United States saw an 18.1% increase in homelessness this year, a dramatic rise driven mostly by a lack of affordable housing as well as devastating natural disasters and a surge of migrants in several parts of the country, federal officials said Friday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Most blame insurance profits and denials alongside the killer in UHC CEO death: poll
item.description: Most Americans believe health insurance profits and coverage denials share responsibility for the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO — although not as much as the person who pulled the trigger, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
